refactorings_applied: DRY: Replace local getErrorMessage() in auto-yes-manager.ts with import from errors.ts
DRY: Replace 5 inline error message extractions in session-cleanup.ts with getErrorMessage() import
SRP/Encapsulation: Add getScheduleWorktreeIds() to schedule-manager.ts to eliminate direct globalThis access
SRP/Encapsulation: Remove direct globalThis.__scheduleManagerStates access from resource-cleanup.ts
Test: Add 3 new tests for getScheduleWorktreeIds() function
Test: Update resource-cleanup tests to use mock instead of globalThis manipulation
changes_made: Replaced local getErrorMessage() in auto-yes-manager.ts with import from errors.ts (DRY principle)
Replaced 5 inline 'error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)' patterns in session-cleanup.ts with getErrorMessage() (DRY principle)
Added getScheduleWorktreeIds() to schedule-manager.ts for proper encapsulation of globalThis state (SRP principle)
Removed direct globalThis.__scheduleManagerStates access from resource-cleanup.ts in favor of the new accessor function
Added 3 new tests for getScheduleWorktreeIds() covering: unique IDs, empty schedules, uninitialized state
Updated resource-cleanup test to use vi.mocked(getScheduleWorktreeIds) instead of globalThis manipulation
commits: 56a71d8: refactor(resource-cleanup): improve code quality and encapsulation (Issue #404)
notes: All 4462 unit tests pass (3 new tests added). ESLint 0 errors, TypeScript 0 errors, build successful. Key improvements: (1) DRY - eliminated duplicate getErrorMessage implementations across 2 files, (2) SRP/Encapsulation - schedule-manager now exposes worktree IDs through a proper API function instead of requiring consumers to access globalThis directly, improving module encapsulation and testability.
